evidence: The paper explicitly ranks root traits by MSE reduction from ranger/randomForest impurity importance. However, their main breeding conclusion - selecting against RAvdiam - rests on r_g = -0.827 and predicted correlated response (CR) efficiency of 112.8%, not on RF importance magnitude. RF rankings are independently validated against genetic (r_g) and phenotypic (r_p) correlations: "The traits with the greatest variable importance (Tips under HN and Xsings under LN) identified by ML also had the largest r_g and r_p to SWt." While mismatches are noted ("two-trait position switch"), genetic correlations provide corroboration via a mechanistically different method. The paper's substantive recommendations depend on heritability and correlated-response calculations, which do not depend on impurity importance.
